channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from multiple sources

item.title: 🇰🇼🛢️ Kuwait Records Zero Crude Oil Exports for First Time Since 1991 Gulf War
item.description: TankerTrackers confirms Kuwait exported no crude oil in April due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, marking the first month without exports since the 1991 Gulf War. The disruption highlights growing impacts on Gulf oil producers from the Iran-US conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇺🇯🇵 Australia and Japan Deepen Ties with Focus on Economic Security and Supply Chains
item.description: Leaders of Australia and Japan pledged to build resilient supply chains for energy, critical minerals, and food amid concerns over economic coercion. The agreement counters China's industrial leverage and addresses Hormuz disruption impacts.

item.title: 🇨🇳⚖️ China to Expand AI Judicial System After Shenzhen Courts Show 50% Efficiency Gain
item.description: Shenzhen judges processed 50% more cases in 2025 using an AI-powered judicial system, prompting expansion to dozens more Chinese cities. The system covers 85 judicial procedures using domain-specific large language models.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 US Republicans Criticize German Troop Withdrawal Plan
item.description: Chairs of House and Senate armed services committees warn that withdrawing 5,000 US service personnel from Germany would undermine deterrence against Russia at a critical moment.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇸🇿 Taiwan President Visits Eswatini After China Cancels Trip
item.description: President Tsai Ing-wen arrived in Taiwan's African ally days after Beijing blocked his travel plans. China denounced the visit as a "stowaway-style escape farce."

item.title: 🇳🇬🇿🇦 Nigeria Summons South African Envoy Over Attacks on Nationals
item.description: Abuja protested to Pretoria following a wave of anti-immigrant violence targeting Nigerian citizens in South Africa. Some protests have turned violent amid rising xenophobic tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇫🇮 Ukraine Offers Finland Drone Technology Partnership
item.description: President Zelenskiy proposed a drone deal with Finland, offering to share combat technology and expertise developed during the four-year war with Russia.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇪🇺🇺🇦 UK to Join EU 78 Billion Pound Ukraine Loan Package
item.description: Britain is set to enter talks to join the European Union massive loan to Ukraine, deepening European defense ties amid rising US pressure for burden sharing.

item.title: 🇸🇪🚢 Swedish Coast Guard Seizes Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
item.description: Swedish authorities seized a tanker in the Baltic Sea believed to be part of Russia shadow fleet, the latest in a string of similar actions targeting sanctions evasion.

item.title: 🇸🇰🇷🇺 Slovakia PM Fico to Attend Moscow Victory Day Parade
item.description: Robert Fico will be among only a handful of global leaders at Russia scaled-back Victory Day parade, highlighting Slovakia divergent stance within the EU and NATO.

item.title: 🇪🇺💰 ECB Warns Euro Zone Recession Risks from Middle East Conflict
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Stournaras said recession concerns are real and justified if the Middle East conflict continues, with Iran war talks key for ECB monetary policy.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Jerusalem Post Live: Trump Calls Iran Peace Plan "Unacceptable", US Escorts Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: Trump rejected Iran's 14-point peace proposal and announced US military will escort stranded vessels through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday. Israel plans major F-35 and F-15 fleet expansion.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe's Fear: Putin's "Window of Opportunity" to Test NATO Commitment
item.description: European governments worry the next two years present Russia with the ideal moment to probe Western resolve on NATO defense commitments amid US distraction with Iran.

item.title: 🇫🇮🇸🇪 Nordic Nuclear Shift: Finland Opens, Sweden Seeks Own Umbrella
item.description: Finland and Sweden, long neutral outside military alliances, are rethinking nuclear deterrence strategies. Finland opens to nuclear discussions while Sweden explores independent deterrent options.

item.title: 🚢 Trump Announces "Project Freedom" to Escort Ships Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US will launch a military-backed operation to guide stranded commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, with 15,000 personnel and over 100 aircraft committed to the effort as the Iran conflict continues.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indian Rupee and Bonds Face Pressure from Prolonged US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Stalled efforts to resolve the US-Iran conflict are keeping oil prices elevated, straining capital flows into energy-importing India as the rupee and government bonds face fresh pressure.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ Output Increase Symbolic but Significant Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: The seven remaining OPEC+ members agreed to increase output for a third straight month in June, though analysts say the boost is largely symbolic given supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: ⛪ Rubio to Meet Pope Leo in Rome Following Trump Criticism
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to Italy this week to meet Pope Leo, weeks after President Trump criticized the pontiff, in an effort to repair diplomatic relations.

item.title: 🌏 ADB Launches Finance Scheme for Critical Mineral Supply Chains
item.description: The Asian Development Bank unveiled a new financing mechanism to help Asia-Pacific nations develop supply chains for critical minerals used in clean energy, batteries, and electric vehicles.

item.title: 📈 Asian Stocks Hit Record High as Tech Rally Offsets Middle East Tensions
item.description: Asian equities excluding Japan reached an all-time high as technology stocks rallied, with investors shrugging off ongoing geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Hezbollah suffers heavy losses in battle to reverse its fortunes in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel has occupied a chunk of southern Lebanon and displaced thousands of Shi'ite Muslim constituents, with Hezbollah paying a steep price in its efforts to regain ground and influence in the region.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇦⚠️ Two US troops missing during African Lion military exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two U.S. service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near the city of Tan Tan in southern Morocco, according to U.S. Africa Command and Moroccan authorities.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇺🇸🛡️ Poland says no signals of delays in US Patriot missile deliveries
item.description: Warsaw has not received any indications that possible delays in American weapons deliveries to Poland would affect Patriot air defense systems, the Polish Defence Minister stated amid broader concerns about US military aid flows.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇹🇼🇸🇿 US praises Taiwan as trusted partner ahead of President Lai's Africa visit
item.description: The U.S. State Department called Taiwan a "trusted and capable" partner and praised Taipei's global relationships, including with Eswatini, as President Lai Ching-te visits the southern African kingdom.

item.title: 🇺🇸💨⚠️ Trump administration halts US wind farm projects citing national security
item.description: President Trump's administration has brought U.S. onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns, the Financial Times reported, marking a significant shift in American energy policy.
